input: question: Show the stadium name and the number of concerts in each stadium. | schema: stadium ( Stadium_ID [PK], Name ) ; concert ( concert_ID [PK], Stadium_ID ) | foreign keys: concert.Stadium_ID -> stadium.Stadium_ID
output: step1: SCAN | table: concert | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: stadium | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.stadium_id = T2.stadium_id || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.stadium_id | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: PROJECT | columns: T2.name, COUNT(*)

input: question: Show the stadium name and capacity with most number of concerts in year 2014 or after. | schema: stadium ( Stadium_ID [PK], Name, Capacity ) ; concert ( concert_ID [PK], Stadium_ID, Year ) | foreign keys: concert.Stadium_ID -> stadium.Stadium_ID

sql: SELECT T2.name ,  T2.capacity FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id  =  T2.stadium_id WHERE T1.year  >=  2014 GROUP BY T2.stadium_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: Find the model of the car whose weight is below the average weight. | schema: car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Weight ) | foreign keys: cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId

input: question: Find the name of the makers that produced some cars in the year of 1970? | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) ; car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Year ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id ; car_names.Model -> model_list.Model ; cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Id = T2.Maker || step4: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.model = T3.model || step6: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T4 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T3.MakeId = T4.id || step8: FILTER | condition: T4.year = '1970' || step9: PROJECT | columns: T1.Maker || step10: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T3 ON T2.model  =  T3.model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T4 ON T3.MakeId  =  T4.id WHERE T4.year  =  '1970';

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T3 ON T2.model  =  T3.model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T4 ON T3.MakeId  =  T4.id WHERE T4.year  =  '1970';

input: question: Find the make and production time of the cars that were produced in the earliest year? | schema: car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model, Make ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Year ) | foreign keys: cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Id = T2.MakeId || step4: SUBQUERY | Evaluate scalar subquery || step5: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA || step6: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute MIN(YEAR) || step7: PROJECT | columns: MIN(YEAR) || step8: FILTER | condition: T1.Year = (SELECT MIN(YEAR) FROM CARS_DATA) || step9: PROJECT | columns: T2.Make, T1.Year

input: question: Which distinct car models are the produced after 1980? | schema: model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) ; car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Year ) | foreign keys: car_names.Model -> model_list.Model ; cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.model = T2.model || step4: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.MakeId = T3.id || step6: FILTER | condition: T3.year > 1980 || step7: PROJECT | columns: T1.model || step8: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.model FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.model  =  T2.model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T3 ON T2.MakeId  =  T3.id WHERE T3.year  >  1980;

sql: SELECT T1.Continent ,  count(*) FROM CONTINENTS AS T1 JOIN COUNTRIES AS T2 ON T1.ContId  =  T2.continent JOIN car_makers AS T3 ON T2.CountryId  =  T3.Country GROUP BY T1.Continent;

input: question: What is the name of the country with the most car makers? | schema: countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) | foreign keys: car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId
output: step1: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: COUNTRIES | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Country = T2.CountryId || step4: AGGREGATE | GROUP BY [T1.Country] || step5: PROJECT | columns: T2.CountryName || step6: SORT | by: COUNT(*) DESC || step7: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T2.CountryName FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN COUNTRIES AS T2 ON T1.Country  =  T2.CountryId GROUP BY T1.Country ORDER BY Count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

input: question: How many car models are produced by each maker ? Only list the count and the maker full name . | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, FullName, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id
output: step1: SCAN | table: model_list | alias: t1 || step2: SCAN | table: car_makers | alias: t2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.maker = t2.id || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: t2.id | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: PROJECT | columns: COUNT(*), t2.fullname
sql: select count(*) ,  t2.fullname from model_list as t1 join car_makers as t2 on t1.maker  =  t2.id group by t2.id;

input: question: What is the number of car models that are produced by each maker and what is the id and full name of each maker? | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, FullName, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id
output: step1: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Maker = T2.Id || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: T2.id | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: PROJECT | columns: COUNT(*), T2.FullName, T2.id
sql: SELECT Count(*) ,  T2.FullName ,  T2.id FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.Maker  =  T2.Id GROUP BY T2.id;

input: question: What are the countries having at least one car maker? List name and id. | schema: countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) | foreign keys: car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId
output: step1: SCAN | table: COUNTRIES | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.CountryId = T2.Country || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.CountryId | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) >= 1 || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.CountryName, T1.CountryId
sql: SELECT T1.CountryName ,  T1.CountryId FROM COUNTRIES AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.CountryId  =  T2.Country GROUP BY T1.CountryId HAVING count(*)  >=  1;

input: question: Which countries in europe have at least 3 car manufacturers? | schema: continents ( ContId [PK], Continent ) ; countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) | foreign keys: countries.Continent -> continents.ContId ; car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId
output: step1: SCAN | table: COUNTRIES | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CONTINENTS | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Continent = T2.ContId || step4: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T1.CountryId = T3.Country || step6: FILTER | condition: T2.Continent = 'europe' || step7: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.CountryName | compute: COUNT(*) || step8: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) >= 3 || step9: PROJECT | columns: T1.CountryName
sql: SELECT T1.CountryName FROM COUNTRIES AS T1 JOIN CONTINENTS AS T2 ON T1.Continent  =  T2.ContId JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T3 ON T1.CountryId  =  T3.Country WHERE T2.Continent  =  'europe' GROUP BY T1.CountryName HAVING count(*)  >=  3;

input: question: What are the names of all European countries with at least 3 manufacturers? | schema: continents ( ContId [PK], Continent ) ; countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) | foreign keys: countries.Continent -> continents.ContId ; car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId
output: step1: SCAN | table: COUNTRIES | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CONTINENTS | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Continent = T2.ContId || step4: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T1.CountryId = T3.Country || step6: FILTER | condition: T2.Continent = 'europe' || step7: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.CountryName | compute: COUNT(*) || step8: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) >= 3 || step9: PROJECT | columns: T1.CountryName
sql: SELECT T1.CountryName FROM COUNTRIES AS T1 JOIN CONTINENTS AS T2 ON T1.Continent  =  T2.ContId JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T3 ON T1.CountryId  =  T3.Country WHERE T2.Continent  =  'europe' GROUP BY T1.CountryName HAVING count(*)  >=  3;

input: question: What is the maximum horsepower and the make of the car models with 3 cylinders? | schema: car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model, Make ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Cylinders, Horsepower ) | foreign keys: cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId

output: step1: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Id = T2.Maker || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.Id | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 3 || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.FullName, T1.Id
sql: SELECT T1.FullName ,  T1.Id FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Id HAVING count(*)  >  3;

input: question: What are the names and ids of all makers with more than 3 models? | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, FullName, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id
output: step1: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Id = T2.Maker || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.Id | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 3 || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.FullName, T1.Id
sql: SELECT T1.FullName ,  T1.Id FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Id HAVING count(*)  >  3;

input: question: Which distinctive models are produced by maker with the full name General Motors or weighing more than 3500? | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, FullName, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) ; car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Weight ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id ; car_names.Model -> model_list.Model ; cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Model = T2.Model || step4: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.Maker = T3.Id || step6: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T4 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T1.MakeId = T4.Id || step8: FILTER | condition: T3.FullName = 'General Motors' OR T4.weight > 3500 || step9: PROJECT | columns: T2.Model || step10: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Model  =  T2.Model JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T3 ON T2.Maker  =  T3.Id JOIN CARS_DATA AS T4 ON T1.MakeId  =  T4.Id WHERE T3.FullName  =  'General Motors' OR T4.weight  >  3500;

output: step1: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Model = T2.Model || step4: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.Maker = T3.Id || step6: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T4 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T1.MakeId = T4.Id || step8: FILTER | condition: T3.FullName = 'General Motors' OR T4.weight > 3500 || step9: PROJECT | columns: T2.Model || step10: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Model  =  T2.Model JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T3 ON T2.Maker  =  T3.Id JOIN CARS_DATA AS T4 ON T1.MakeId  =  T4.Id WHERE T3.FullName  =  'General Motors' OR T4.weight  >  3500;

input: question: How many cars have a larger accelerate than the car with the largest horsepower? | schema: cars_data ( Id [PK], Horsepower, Accelerate ) | foreign keys: none
output: step1: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA || step2: SUBQUERY | Evaluate scalar subquery || step3: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA || step4: PROJECT | columns: Accelerate || step5: SORT | by: Horsepower DESC || step6: LIMIT | value: 1 || step7: FILTER | condition: Accelerate > (SELECT Accelerate FROM CARS_DATA ORDER BY Horsepower DESC LIMIT 1) || step8: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute COUNT(*) || step9: PROJECT | columns: COUNT(*)
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CARS_DATA WHERE Accelerate  >  ( SELECT Accelerate FROM CARS_DATA ORDER BY Horsepower DESC LIMIT 1 );

input: question: Among the cars with more than lowest horsepower, which ones do not have more than 3 cylinders? List the car makeid and make name. | schema: car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model, Make ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Cylinders, Horsepower ) | foreign keys: cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Id = T2.MakeId || step4: SUBQUERY | Evaluate scalar subquery || step5: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA || step6: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute MIN(Horsepower) || step7: PROJECT | columns: MIN(Horsepower) || step8: FILTER | condition: T1.Horsepower > (SELECT MIN(Horsepower) FROM CARS_DATA) AND T1.Cylinders <= 3 || step9: PROJECT | columns: T2.MakeId, T2.Make
sql: SELECT T2.MakeId ,  T2.Make FROM CARS_DATA AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.MakeId WHERE T1.Horsepower  >  (SELECT min(Horsepower) FROM CARS_DATA) AND T1.Cylinders  <=  3;

input: question: Among the cars that do not have the minimum horsepower , what are the make ids and names of all those with less than 4 cylinders ? | schema: car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model, Make ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Cylinders, Horsepower ) | foreign keys: cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: cars_data | alias: t1 || step2: SCAN | table: car_names | alias: t2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.id = t2.makeid || step4: SUBQUERY | Evaluate scalar subquery || step5: SCAN | table: cars_data || step6: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute MIN(horsepower) || step7: PROJECT | columns: MIN(horsepower) || step8: FILTER | condition: t1.horsepower > (SELECT MIN(horsepower) FROM cars_data) AND t1.cylinders < 4 || step9: PROJECT | columns: t2.makeid, t2.make
sql: select t2.makeid ,  t2.make from cars_data as t1 join car_names as t2 on t1.id  =  t2.makeid where t1.horsepower  >  (select min(horsepower) from cars_data) and t1.cylinders  <  4;

input: question: Which models are lighter than 3500 but not built by the 'Ford Motor Company'? | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, FullName, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) ; car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Weight ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id ; car_names.Model -> model_list.Model ; cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Model = T2.Model || step4: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.MakeId = T3.Id || step6: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T4 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T1.Maker = T4.Id || step8: FILTER | condition: T3.weight < 3500 AND T4.FullName <> 'Ford Motor Company' || step9: PROJECT | columns: T1.model || step10: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.model FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Model  =  T2.Model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T3 ON T2.MakeId  =  T3.Id JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T4 ON T1.Maker  =  T4.Id WHERE T3.weight  <  3500 AND T4.FullName != 'Ford Motor Company';

input: question: What are the different models wthat are lighter than 3500 but were not built by the Ford Motor Company? | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, FullName, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) ; car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) ; cars_data ( Id [PK], Weight ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id ; car_names.Model -> model_list.Model ; cars_data.Id -> car_names.MakeId
output: step1: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Model = T2.Model || step4: SCAN | table: CARS_DATA | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.MakeId = T3.Id || step6: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T4 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T1.Maker = T4.Id || step8: FILTER | condition: T3.weight < 3500 AND T4.FullName <> 'Ford Motor Company' || step9: PROJECT | columns: T1.model || step10: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.model FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Model  =  T2.Model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T3 ON T2.MakeId  =  T3.Id JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T4 ON T1.Maker  =  T4.Id WHERE T3.weight  <  3500 AND T4.FullName != 'Ford Motor Company';

input: question: What are the name of the countries where there is not a single car maker? | schema: countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) | foreign keys: car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of EXCEPT || step2: SCAN | table: countries || step3: PROJECT | columns: CountryName || step4: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of EXCEPT || step5: SCAN | table: countries | alias: T1 || step6: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T2 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.countryId = T2.Country || step8: PROJECT | columns: T1.CountryName || step9: EXCEPT | Keep rows in LEFT not present in RIGHT
sql: SELECT CountryName FROM countries EXCEPT SELECT T1.CountryName FROM countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.countryId  =  T2.Country;

input: question: What are the names of the countries with no car makers? | schema: countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) | foreign keys: car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of EXCEPT || step2: SCAN | table: countries || step3: PROJECT | columns: CountryName || step4: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of EXCEPT || step5: SCAN | table: countries | alias: T1 || step6: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T2 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.countryId = T2.Country || step8: PROJECT | columns: T1.CountryName || step9: EXCEPT | Keep rows in LEFT not present in RIGHT
sql: SELECT CountryName FROM countries EXCEPT SELECT T1.CountryName FROM countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.countryId  =  T2.Country;

input: question: Which are the car makers which produce at least 2 models and more than 3 car makers ? List the id and the maker . | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) ; car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id ; car_names.Model -> model_list.Model
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: car_makers | alias: t1 || step3: SCAN | table: model_list | alias: t2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.id = t2.maker || step5: AGGREGATE | group_by: t1.id | compute: COUNT(*) || step6: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) >= 2 || step7: PROJECT | columns: t1.id, t1.maker || step8: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step9: SCAN | table: car_makers | alias: t1 || step10: SCAN | table: model_list | alias: t2 || step11: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.id = t2.maker || step12: SCAN | table: car_names | alias: t3 || step13: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t3 | on: t2.model = t3.model || step14: AGGREGATE | group_by: t1.id | compute: COUNT(*) || step15: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 3 || step16: PROJECT | columns: t1.id, t1.maker || step17: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: select t1.id ,  t1.maker from car_makers as t1 join model_list as t2 on t1.id  =  t2.maker group by t1.id having count(*)  >=  2 intersect select t1.id ,  t1.maker from car_makers as t1 join model_list as t2 on t1.id  =  t2.maker join car_names as t3 on t2.model  =  t3.model group by t1.id having count(*)  >  3;

input: question: What are the ids and makers of all car makers that produce at least 2 models and make more than 3 cars? | schema: car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) ; car_names ( MakeId [PK], Model ) | foreign keys: model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id ; car_names.Model -> model_list.Model
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Id = T2.Maker || step5: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.Id | compute: COUNT(*) || step6: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) >= 2 || step7: PROJECT | columns: T1.Id, T1.Maker || step8: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step9: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T1 || step10: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T2 || step11: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Id = T2.Maker || step12: SCAN | table: CAR_NAMES | alias: T3 || step13: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.model = T3.model || step14: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.Id | compute: COUNT(*) || step15: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 3 || step16: PROJECT | columns: T1.Id, T1.Maker || step17: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT T1.Id ,  T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Id HAVING count(*)  >=  2 INTERSECT SELECT T1.Id ,  T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T3 ON T2.model  =  T3.model GROUP BY T1.Id HAVING count(*)  >  3;

input: question: What are the id and names of the countries which have more than 3 car makers or produce the 'fiat' model? | schema: countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) | foreign keys: car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId ; model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of UNION || step2: SCAN | table: Countries | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.CountryId = T2.Country || step5: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.countryId | compute: COUNT(*) || step6: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 3 || step7: PROJECT | columns: T1.countryId, T1.CountryName || step8: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of UNION || step9: SCAN | table: Countries | alias: T1 || step10: SCAN | table: CAR_MAKERS | alias: T2 || step11: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.CountryId = T2.Country || step12: SCAN | table: MODEL_LIST | alias: T3 || step13: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.Id = T3.Maker || step14: FILTER | condition: T3.Model = 'fiat' || step15: PROJECT | columns: T1.countryId, T1.CountryName || step16: UNION | Merge both result sets (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT T1.countryId ,  T1.CountryName FROM Countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.CountryId  =  T2.Country GROUP BY T1.countryId HAVING count(*)  >  3 UNION SELECT T1.countryId ,  T1.CountryName FROM Countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.CountryId  =  T2.Country JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T3 ON T2.Id  =  T3.Maker WHERE T3.Model  =  'fiat';

input: question: What are the ids and names of all countries that either have more than 3 car makers or produce fiat model ? | schema: countries ( CountryId [PK], CountryName, Continent ) ; car_makers ( Id [PK], Maker, Country ) ; model_list ( ModelId [PK], Maker, Model ) | foreign keys: car_makers.Country -> countries.CountryId ; model_list.Maker -> car_makers.Id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of UNION || step2: SCAN | table: countries | alias: t1 || step3: SCAN | table: car_makers | alias: t2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.countryid = t2.country || step5: AGGREGATE | group_by: t1.countryid | compute: COUNT(*) || step6: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 3 || step7: PROJECT | columns: t1.countryid, t1.countryname || step8: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of UNION || step9: SCAN | table: countries | alias: t1 || step10: SCAN | table: car_makers | alias: t2 || step11: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.countryid = t2.country || step12: SCAN | table: model_list | alias: t3 || step13: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t3 | on: t2.id = t3.maker || step14: FILTER | condition: t3.model = 'fiat' || step15: PROJECT | columns: t1.countryid, t1.countryname || step16: UNION | Merge both result sets (remove duplicates)
sql: select t1.countryid ,  t1.countryname from countries as t1 join car_makers as t2 on t1.countryid  =  t2.country group by t1.countryid having count(*)  >  3 union select t1.countryid ,  t1.countryname from countries as t1 join car_makers as t2 on t1.countryid  =  t2.country join model_list as t3 on t2.id  =  t3.maker where t3.model  =  'fiat';

input: question: find the id, name and age for visitors who visited some museums more than once. | schema: visitor ( ID [PK], Name, Age ) ; visit ( Museum_ID [PK], visitor_ID ) | foreign keys: visit.visitor_ID -> visitor.ID
output: step1: SCAN | table: visitor | alias: t1 || step2: SCAN | table: visit | alias: t2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.id = t2.visitor_id || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: t1.id | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 1 || step6: PROJECT | columns: t1.id, t1.name, t1.age
sql: SELECT t1.id ,  t1.name ,  t1.age FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id  =  t2.visitor_id GROUP BY t1.id HAVING count(*)  >  1

input: question: What are the id, name and membership level of visitors who have spent the largest amount of money in total in all museum tickets? | schema: visitor ( ID [PK], Name, Level_of_membership ) ; visit ( Museum_ID [PK], visitor_ID, Total_spent ) | foreign keys: visit.visitor_ID -> visitor.ID

sql: SELECT t2.visitor_id ,  t1.name ,  t1.Level_of_membership FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id  =  t2.visitor_id GROUP BY t2.visitor_id ORDER BY sum(t2.Total_spent) DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: What is the name of the visitor who visited both a museum opened before 2009 and a museum opened after 2011? | schema: museum ( Museum_ID [PK], Name, Open_Year ) ; visitor ( ID [PK], Name ) ; visit ( Museum_ID [PK], visitor_ID ) | foreign keys: visit.visitor_ID -> visitor.ID ; visit.Museum_ID -> museum.Museum_ID
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: visitor | alias: t1 || step3: SCAN | table: visit | alias: t2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.id = t2.visitor_id || step5: SCAN | table: museum | alias: t3 || step6: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t3 | on: t3.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID || step7: FILTER | condition: t3.open_year < 2009 || step8: PROJECT | columns: t1.name || step9: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step10: SCAN | table: visitor | alias: t1 || step11: SCAN | table: visit | alias: t2 || step12: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.id = t2.visitor_id || step13: SCAN | table: museum | alias: t3 || step14: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t3 | on: t3.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID || step15: FILTER | condition: t3.open_year > 2011 || step16: PROJECT | columns: t1.name || step17: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id  =  t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID  =  t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year  <  2009 INTERSECT SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id  =  t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID  =  t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year  >  2011

input: question: Find the number of visitors who did not visit any museum opened after 2010. | schema: museum ( Museum_ID [PK], Open_Year ) ; visitor ( ID [PK] ) ; visit ( Museum_ID [PK], visitor_ID ) | foreign keys: visit.visitor_ID -> visitor.ID ; visit.Museum_ID -> museum.Museum_ID
output: step1: SCAN | table: visitor || step2: SUBQUERY | Evaluate NOT IN subquery || step3: SCAN | table: museum | alias: t1 || step4: SCAN | table: visit | alias: t2 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID || step6: FILTER | condition: t1.open_year > 2010 || step7: PROJECT | columns: t2.visitor_id || step8: FILTER | condition: NOT id IN (SELECT t2.visitor_id FROM museum AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID WHERE t1.open_year > 2010) || step9: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute COUNT(*) || step10: PROJECT | columns: COUNT(*)
sql: SELECT count(*) FROM visitor WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT t2.visitor_id FROM museum AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.Museum_ID  =  t2.Museum_ID WHERE t1.open_year  >  2010)

input: question: What are the country code and first name of the players who won in both tourney WTA Championships and Australian Open? | schema: players ( player_id [PK], first_name, country_code ) ; matches ( loser_id, tourney_name, winner_id ) | foreign keys: matches.winner_id -> players.player_id ; matches.loser_id -> players.player_id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: players | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: matches | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.player_id = T2.winner_id || step5: FILTER | condition: T2.tourney_name = 'WTA Championships' || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.country_code, T1.first_name || step7: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step8: SCAN | table: players | alias: T1 || step9: SCAN | table: matches | alias: T2 || step10: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.player_id = T2.winner_id || step11: FILTER | condition: T2.tourney_name = 'Australian Open' || step12: PROJECT | columns: T1.country_code, T1.first_name || step13: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)

input: question: What are the death and injury situations caused by the ship with tonnage 't'? | schema: ship ( lost_in_battle, id [PK], tonnage ) ; death ( caused_by_ship_id, id [PK], killed, injured ) | foreign keys: death.caused_by_ship_id -> ship.id
output: step1: SCAN | table: death | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: ship | alias: t2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: T1.caused_by_ship_id = T2.id || step4: FILTER | condition: T2.tonnage = 't' || step5: PROJECT | columns: T1.killed, T1.injured

input: question: What are the different ids and names of the battles that lost any 'Brig' type shipes? | schema: battle ( id [PK], name ) ; ship ( lost_in_battle, id [PK], name, ship_type ) | foreign keys: ship.lost_in_battle -> battle.id
output: step1: SCAN | table: battle | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: ship | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle || step4: FILTER | condition: T2.ship_type = 'Brig' || step5: PROJECT | columns: T1.id, T1.name || step6: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.id ,  T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.ship_type  =  'Brig'

input: question: What are the ids and names of the battles that led to more than 10 people killed in total. | schema: battle ( id [PK], name ) ; ship ( lost_in_battle, id [PK], name ) ; death ( caused_by_ship_id, id [PK], killed ) | foreign keys: ship.lost_in_battle -> battle.id ; death.caused_by_ship_id -> ship.id
output: step1: SCAN | table: battle | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: ship | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle || step4: SCAN | table: death | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.id = T3.caused_by_ship_id || step6: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.id | compute: SUM(T3.killed) || step7: HAVING-FILTER | condition: SUM(T3.killed) > 10 || step8: PROJECT | columns: T1.id, T1.name
sql: SELECT T1.id ,  T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id  =  T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING sum(T3.killed)  >  10

input: question: What is the ship id and name that caused most total injuries? | schema: ship ( lost_in_battle, id [PK], name ) ; death ( caused_by_ship_id, id [PK] ) | foreign keys: death.caused_by_ship_id -> ship.id
output: step1: SCAN | table: death | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: ship | alias: t2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: T1.caused_by_ship_id = T2.id || step4: AGGREGATE | GROUP BY [T2.id] || step5: PROJECT | columns: T2.id, T2.name || step6: SORT | by: COUNT(*) DESC || step7: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T2.id ,  T2.name FROM death AS T1 JOIN ship AS t2 ON T1.caused_by_ship_id  =  T2.id GROUP BY T2.id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: How many battles did not lose any ship with tonnage '225'? | schema: battle ( id [PK] ) ; ship ( lost_in_battle, id [PK], tonnage ) | foreign keys: ship.lost_in_battle -> battle.id
output: step1: SCAN | table: battle || step2: SUBQUERY | Evaluate NOT IN subquery || step3: SCAN | table: ship || step4: FILTER | condition: tonnage = '225' || step5: PROJECT | columns: lost_in_battle || step6: FILTER | condition: NOT id IN (SELECT lost_in_battle FROM ship WHERE tonnage = '225') || step7: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute COUNT(*) || step8: PROJECT | columns: COUNT(*)

input: question: List the name and date the battle that has lost the ship named 'Lettice' and the ship named 'HMS Atalanta' | schema: battle ( id [PK], name, date ) ; ship ( lost_in_battle, id [PK], name ) | foreign keys: ship.lost_in_battle -> battle.id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: battle | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: ship | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle || step5: FILTER | condition: T2.name = 'Lettice' || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.name, T1.date || step7: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step8: SCAN | table: battle | alias: T1 || step9: SCAN | table: ship | alias: T2 || step10: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle || step11: FILTER | condition: T2.name = 'HMS Atalanta' || step12: PROJECT | columns: T1.name, T1.date || step13: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT T1.name ,  T1.date FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.name  =  'Lettice' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name ,  T1.date FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.name  =  'HMS Atalanta'

input: question: Show names, results and bulgarian commanders of the battles with no ships lost in the 'English Channel'. | schema: battle ( id [PK], name, bulgarian_commander, result ) ; ship ( lost_in_battle, id [PK], name, location ) | foreign keys: ship.lost_in_battle -> battle.id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of EXCEPT || step2: SCAN | table: battle || step3: PROJECT | columns: name, RESULT, bulgarian_commander || step4: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of EXCEPT || step5: SCAN | table: battle | alias: T1 || step6: SCAN | table: ship | alias: T2 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.id = T2.lost_in_battle || step8: FILTER | condition: T2.location = 'English Channel' || step9: PROJECT | columns: T1.name, T1.result, T1.bulgarian_commander || step10: EXCEPT | Keep rows in LEFT not present in RIGHT
sql: SELECT name ,  RESULT ,  bulgarian_commander FROM battle EXCEPT SELECT T1.name ,  T1.result ,  T1.bulgarian_commander FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.location  =  'English Channel'

output: step1: SCAN | table: Degree_Programs | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: Departments | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.department_id = T2.department_id || step4: AGGREGATE | GROUP BY [T1.department_id] || step5: PROJECT | columns: T2.department_name, T1.department_id || step6: SORT | by: COUNT(*) DESC || step7: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T2.department_name ,  T1.department_id FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Departments AS T2 ON T1.department_id  =  T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: Who is enrolled in a Bachelor degree program? List the first name, middle name, last name. | schema: Degree_Programs ( degree_program_id [PK], department_id, degree_summary_name ) ; Students ( student_id [PK], current_address_id, permanent_address_id, first_name, middle_name, last_name ) ; Student_Enrolment ( student_enrolment_id [PK], degree_program_id, semester_id, student_id ) | foreign keys: Student_Enrolment.student_id -> Students.student_id ; Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id -> Degree_Programs.degree_program_id

input: question: Find the last name of the students who currently live in the state of North Carolina but have not registered in any degree program. | schema: Addresses ( address_id [PK], state_province_county ) ; Students ( student_id [PK], current_address_id, permanent_address_id, last_name ) ; Student_Enrolment ( student_enrolment_id [PK], degree_program_id, semester_id, student_id ) | foreign keys: Students.permanent_address_id -> Addresses.address_id ; Students.current_address_id -> Addresses.address_id ; Student_Enrolment.student_id -> Students.student_id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of EXCEPT || step2: SCAN | table: Students | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: Addresses | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.current_address_id = T2.address_id || step5: FILTER | condition: T2.state_province_county = 'NorthCarolina' || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.last_name || step7: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of EXCEPT || step8: SCAN | table: Students | alias: T3 || step9: SCAN | table: Student_Enrolment | alias: T4 || step10: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T3.student_id = T4.student_id || step11: PROJECT | columns: T3.last_name || step12: DISTINCT | remove duplicates || step13: EXCEPT | Keep rows in LEFT not present in RIGHT
sql: SELECT T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.current_address_id  =  T2.address_id WHERE T2.state_province_county  =  'NorthCarolina' EXCEPT SELECT DISTINCT T3.last_name FROM Students AS T3 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T4 ON T3.student_id  =  T4.student_id

input: question: What are the last name of the students who live in North Carolina but have not registered in any degree programs? | schema: Addresses ( address_id [PK], state_province_county ) ; Students ( student_id [PK], current_address_id, permanent_address_id, last_name ) ; Student_Enrolment ( student_enrolment_id [PK], degree_program_id, semester_id, student_id ) | foreign keys: Students.permanent_address_id -> Addresses.address_id ; Students.current_address_id -> Addresses.address_id ; Student_Enrolment.student_id -> Students.student_id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of EXCEPT || step2: SCAN | table: Students | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: Addresses | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.current_address_id = T2.address_id || step5: FILTER | condition: T2.state_province_county = 'NorthCarolina' || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.last_name || step7: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of EXCEPT || step8: SCAN | table: Students | alias: T3 || step9: SCAN | table: Student_Enrolment | alias: T4 || step10: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T3.student_id = T4.student_id || step11: PROJECT | columns: T3.last_name || step12: DISTINCT | remove duplicates || step13: EXCEPT | Keep rows in LEFT not present in RIGHT
sql: SELECT T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.current_address_id  =  T2.address_id WHERE T2.state_province_county  =  'NorthCarolina' EXCEPT SELECT DISTINCT T3.last_name FROM Students AS T3 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T4 ON T3.student_id  =  T4.student_id

input: question: Show the date of the transcript which shows the least number of results, also list the id. | schema: Transcripts ( transcript_id [PK], transcript_date ) ; Transcript_Contents ( student_course_id, transcript_id ) | foreign keys: Transcript_Contents.transcript_id -> Transcripts.transcript_id

input: question: Find the semester when both Master students and Bachelor students got enrolled in. | schema: Degree_Programs ( degree_program_id [PK], department_id, degree_summary_name ) ; Student_Enrolment ( student_enrolment_id [PK], degree_program_id, semester_id, student_id ) | foreign keys: Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id -> Degree_Programs.degree_program_id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: Degree_Programs | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: Student_Enrolment | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.degree_program_id = T2.degree_program_id || step5: FILTER | condition: degree_summary_name = 'Master' || step6: PROJECT | columns: T2.semester_id || step7: DISTINCT | remove duplicates || step8: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step9: SCAN | table: Degree_Programs | alias: T1 || step10: SCAN | table: Student_Enrolment | alias: T2 || step11: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.degree_program_id = T2.degree_program_id || step12: FILTER | condition: degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor' || step13: PROJECT | columns: T2.semester_id || step14: DISTINCT | remove duplicates || step15: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.semester_id FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.degree_program_id  =  T2.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name  =  'Master' INTERSECT SELECT DISTINCT T2.semester_id FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.degree_program_id  =  T2.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name  =  'Bachelor'

input: question: What is the id of the semester that had both Masters and Bachelors students enrolled? | schema: Degree_Programs ( degree_program_id [PK], department_id, degree_summary_name ) ; Student_Enrolment ( student_enrolment_id [PK], degree_program_id, semester_id, student_id ) | foreign keys: Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id -> Degree_Programs.degree_program_id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: Degree_Programs | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: Student_Enrolment | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.degree_program_id = T2.degree_program_id || step5: FILTER | condition: degree_summary_name = 'Master' || step6: PROJECT | columns: T2.semester_id || step7: DISTINCT | remove duplicates || step8: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step9: SCAN | table: Degree_Programs | alias: T1 || step10: SCAN | table: Student_Enrolment | alias: T2 || step11: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.degree_program_id = T2.degree_program_id || step12: FILTER | condition: degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor' || step13: PROJECT | columns: T2.semester_id || step14: DISTINCT | remove duplicates || step15: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.semester_id FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.degree_program_id  =  T2.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name  =  'Master' INTERSECT SELECT DISTINCT T2.semester_id FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.degree_program_id  =  T2.degree_program_id WHERE degree_summary_name  =  'Bachelor'

input: question: Find the series name and country of the tv channel that is playing some cartoons directed by Ben Jones and Michael Chang? | schema: TV_Channel ( id [PK], series_name, Country ) ; Cartoon ( id [PK], Directed_by, Channel ) | foreign keys: Cartoon.Channel -> TV_Channel.id
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: TV_Channel | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: cartoon | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.id = T2.Channel || step5: FILTER | condition: T2.directed_by = 'Michael Chang' || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.series_name, T1.country || step7: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step8: SCAN | table: TV_Channel | alias: T1 || step9: SCAN | table: cartoon | alias: T2 || step10: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.id = T2.Channel || step11: FILTER | condition: T2.directed_by = 'Ben Jones' || step12: PROJECT | columns: T1.series_name, T1.country || step13: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT T1.series_name ,  T1.country FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN cartoon AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel WHERE T2.directed_by  =  'Michael Chang' INTERSECT SELECT T1.series_name ,  T1.country FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN cartoon AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel WHERE T2.directed_by  =  'Ben Jones'

sql: SELECT T1.series_name ,  T1.country FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN cartoon AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel WHERE T2.directed_by  =  'Michael Chang' INTERSECT SELECT T1.series_name ,  T1.country FROM TV_Channel AS T1 JOIN cartoon AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.Channel WHERE T2.directed_by  =  'Ben Jones'

input: question: What are the create dates, states, and phone numbers of the votes that were for the contestant named 'Tabatha Gehling'? | schema: CONTESTANTS ( contestant_number [PK], contestant_name ) ; VOTES ( vote_id [PK], phone_number, state, contestant_number, created ) | foreign keys: VOTES.contestant_number -> CONTESTANTS.contestant_number

sql: SELECT T2.created ,  T2.state ,  T2.phone_number FROM contestants AS T1 JOIN votes AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number  =  T2.contestant_number WHERE T1.contestant_name  =  'Tabatha Gehling'

input: question: List the area codes in which voters voted both for the contestant 'Tabatha Gehling' and the contestant 'Kelly Clauss'. | schema: AREA_CODE_STATE ( area_code [PK], state ) ; CONTESTANTS ( contestant_number [PK], contestant_name ) ; VOTES ( vote_id [PK], state, contestant_number ) | foreign keys: VOTES.contestant_number -> CONTESTANTS.contestant_number ; VOTES.state -> AREA_CODE_STATE.state
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: contestants | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: votes | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number || step5: SCAN | table: area_code_state | alias: T3 || step6: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.state = T3.state || step7: FILTER | condition: T1.contestant_name = 'Tabatha Gehling' || step8: PROJECT | columns: T3.area_code || step9: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step10: SCAN | table: contestants | alias: T1 || step11: SCAN | table: votes | alias: T2 || step12: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.contestant_number = T2.contestant_number || step13: SCAN | table: area_code_state | alias: T3 || step14: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T2.state = T3.state || step15: FILTER | condition: T1.contestant_name = 'Kelly Clauss' || step16: PROJECT | columns: T3.area_code || step17: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT T3.area_code FROM contestants AS T1 JOIN votes AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number  =  T2.contestant_number JOIN area_code_state AS T3 ON T2.state  =  T3.state WHERE T1.contestant_name  =  'Tabatha Gehling' INTERSECT SELECT T3.area_code FROM contestants AS T1 JOIN votes AS T2 ON T1.contestant_number  =  T2.contestant_number JOIN area_code_state AS T3 ON T2.state  =  T3.state WHERE T1.contestant_name  =  'Kelly Clauss'

input: question: How many countries speak both English and Dutch? | schema: country ( Code [PK], Name ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SUBQUERY-SCAN | alias: subquery || step2: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step3: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step4: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step6: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "English" || step7: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name || step8: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step9: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step10: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step11: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step12: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "Dutch" || step13: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name || step14: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates) || step15: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute COUNT(*) || step16: PROJECT | columns: COUNT(*)
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "Dutch")

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Region FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" OR T2.Language  =  "Dutch"

output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step4: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "English" OR T2.Language = "Dutch" || step5: PROJECT | columns: T1.Region || step6: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Region FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" OR T2.Language  =  "Dutch"

input: question: What are the countries where either English or Dutch is the official language ? | schema: country ( Code [PK], Name ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language, IsOfficial ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of UNION || step2: SCAN | table: country | alias: t1 || step3: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: t2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.code = t2.countrycode || step5: FILTER | condition: t2.language = "english" AND isofficial = "t" || step6: PROJECT | columns: t1.name || step7: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of UNION || step8: SCAN | table: country | alias: t1 || step9: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: t2 || step10: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.code = t2.countrycode || step11: FILTER | condition: t2.language = "dutch" AND isofficial = "t" || step12: PROJECT | columns: t1.name || step13: UNION | Merge both result sets (remove duplicates)
sql: select t1.name from country as t1 join countrylanguage as t2 on t1.code  =  t2.countrycode where t2.language  =  "english" and isofficial  =  "t" union select t1.name from country as t1 join countrylanguage as t2 on t1.code  =  t2.countrycode where t2.language  =  "dutch" and isofficial  =  "t"

input: question: Which countries have either English or Dutch as an official language? | schema: country ( Code [PK] ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language, IsOfficial ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of UNION || step2: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step3: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step4: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step5: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "English" AND IsOfficial = "T" || step6: PROJECT | columns: * || step7: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of UNION || step8: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step9: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step10: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step11: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "Dutch" AND IsOfficial = "T" || step12: PROJECT | columns: * || step13: UNION | Merge both result sets (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT * FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" AND IsOfficial  =  "T" UNION SELECT * FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "Dutch" AND IsOfficial  =  "T"

input: question: Which language is the most popular on the Asian continent? | schema: country ( Code [PK], Continent ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step4: FILTER | condition: T1.Continent = "Asia" || step5: AGGREGATE | GROUP BY [T2.Language] || step6: PROJECT | columns: T2.Language || step7: SORT | by: COUNT(*) DESC || step8: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent  =  "Asia" GROUP BY T2.Language ORDER BY COUNT (*) DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: What is the language that is used by the largest number of Asian nations? | schema: country ( Code [PK], Continent ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step4: FILTER | condition: T1.Continent = "Asia" || step5: AGGREGATE | GROUP BY [T2.Language] || step6: PROJECT | columns: T2.Language || step7: SORT | by: COUNT(*) DESC || step8: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.Continent  =  "Asia" GROUP BY T2.Language ORDER BY COUNT (*) DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: Which languages are spoken by only one country in republic governments? | schema: country ( Code [PK], GovernmentForm ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step4: FILTER | condition: T1.GovernmentForm = "Republic" || step5: AGGREGATE | group_by: T2.Language | compute: COUNT(*) || step6: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) = 1 || step7: PROJECT | columns: T2.Language
sql: SELECT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.GovernmentForm  =  "Republic" GROUP BY T2.Language HAVING COUNT(*)  =  1

input: question: What languages are only used by a single country with a republic government? | schema: country ( Code [PK], GovernmentForm ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step4: FILTER | condition: T1.GovernmentForm = "Republic" || step5: AGGREGATE | group_by: T2.Language | compute: COUNT(*) || step6: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) = 1 || step7: PROJECT | columns: T2.Language
sql: SELECT T2.Language FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T1.GovernmentForm  =  "Republic" GROUP BY T2.Language HAVING COUNT(*)  =  1

input: question: Find the city with the largest population that uses English. | schema: city ( ID [PK], Name, CountryCode, Population ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: none
output: step1: SCAN | table: city | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode || step4: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "English" || step5: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name, T1.Population || step6: SORT | by: T1.Population DESC || step7: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T1.Name ,  T1.Population FROM city AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" ORDER BY T1.Population DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: What is the most populace city that speaks English? | schema: city ( ID [PK], Name, CountryCode, Population ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: none
output: step1: SCAN | table: city | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.CountryCode = T2.CountryCode || step4: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "English" || step5: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name, T1.Population || step6: SORT | by: T1.Population DESC || step7: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T1.Name ,  T1.Population FROM city AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.CountryCode  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" ORDER BY T1.Population DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: Find the name, population and expected life length of asian country with the largest area? | schema: country ( Code [PK], Name, Continent, SurfaceArea, Population, LifeExpectancy ) | foreign keys: none

input: question: What is average life expectancy in the countries where English is not the official language? | schema: country ( Code [PK], Name, LifeExpectancy ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language, IsOfficial ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country || step2: SUBQUERY | Evaluate NOT IN subquery || step3: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step4: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step6: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "English" AND T2.IsOfficial = "T" || step7: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name || step8: FILTER | condition: NOT Name IN (SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language = "English" AND T2.IsOfficial = "T") || step9: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute AVG(LifeExpectancy) || step10: PROJECT | columns: AVG(LifeExpectancy)
sql: SELECT avg(LifeExpectancy) FROM country WHERE Name NOT IN (SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" AND T2.IsOfficial  =  "T")

sql: SELECT avg(LifeExpectancy) FROM country WHERE Name NOT IN (SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language  =  "English" AND T2.IsOfficial  =  "T")

output: step1: SCAN | table: country || step2: SUBQUERY | Evaluate NOT IN subquery || step3: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step4: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step6: FILTER | condition: T2.Language = "English" || step7: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name || step8: FILTER | condition: NOT Name IN (SELECT T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode WHERE T2.Language = "English") || step9: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute SUM(Population) || step10: PROJECT | columns: SUM(Population)

input: question: What are the countries that have greater surface area than any country in Europe? | schema: country ( Code [PK], Name, Continent, SurfaceArea ) | foreign keys: none
output: step1: SCAN | table: country || step2: SUBQUERY | Evaluate scalar subquery || step3: SCAN | table: country || step4: FILTER | condition: Continent = "Europe" || step5: AGGREGATE | Scalar aggregate (no GROUP BY)  ->  compute MIN(SurfaceArea) || step6: PROJECT | columns: MIN(SurfaceArea) || step7: FILTER | condition: SurfaceArea > (SELECT MIN(SurfaceArea) FROM country WHERE Continent = "Europe") || step8: PROJECT | columns: Name

input: question: What are the country codes for countries that do not speak English? | schema: countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: none
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of EXCEPT || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage || step3: PROJECT | columns: CountryCode || step4: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of EXCEPT || step5: SCAN | table: countrylanguage || step6: FILTER | condition: LANGUAGE = "English" || step7: PROJECT | columns: CountryCode || step8: EXCEPT | Keep rows in LEFT not present in RIGHT

input: question: Which cities are in European countries where English is not the official language? | schema: city ( ID [PK], Name, CountryCode ) ; country ( Code [PK], Name, Continent ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language, IsOfficial ) | foreign keys: city.CountryCode -> country.Code ; countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: city | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T2.CountryCode = T1.Code || step4: SUBQUERY | Evaluate NOT IN subquery || step5: SCAN | table: country | alias: T3 || step6: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T4 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T3.Code = T4.CountryCode || step8: FILTER | condition: T4.IsOfficial = 'T' AND T4.Language = 'English' || step9: PROJECT | columns: T3.Name || step10: FILTER | condition: T1.Continent = 'Europe' AND NOT T1.Name IN (SELECT T3.Name FROM country AS T3 JOIN countrylanguage AS T4 ON T3.Code = T4.CountryCode WHERE T4.IsOfficial = 'T' AND T4.Language = 'English') || step11: PROJECT | columns: T2.Name || step12: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T2.CountryCode  =  T1.Code WHERE T1.Continent  =  'Europe' AND T1.Name NOT IN (SELECT T3.Name FROM country AS T3 JOIN countrylanguage AS T4 ON T3.Code  =  T4.CountryCode WHERE T4.IsOfficial  =  'T' AND T4.Language  =  'English')

input: question: What are the names of cities in Europe for which English is not the official language? | schema: city ( ID [PK], Name, CountryCode ) ; country ( Code [PK], Name, Continent ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language, IsOfficial ) | foreign keys: city.CountryCode -> country.Code ; countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: city | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T2.CountryCode = T1.Code || step4: SUBQUERY | Evaluate NOT IN subquery || step5: SCAN | table: country | alias: T3 || step6: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T4 || step7: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T4 | on: T3.Code = T4.CountryCode || step8: FILTER | condition: T4.IsOfficial = 'T' AND T4.Language = 'English' || step9: PROJECT | columns: T3.Name || step10: FILTER | condition: T1.Continent = 'Europe' AND NOT T1.Name IN (SELECT T3.Name FROM country AS T3 JOIN countrylanguage AS T4 ON T3.Code = T4.CountryCode WHERE T4.IsOfficial = 'T' AND T4.Language = 'English') || step11: PROJECT | columns: T2.Name || step12: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T2.CountryCode  =  T1.Code WHERE T1.Continent  =  'Europe' AND T1.Name NOT IN (SELECT T3.Name FROM country AS T3 JOIN countrylanguage AS T4 ON T3.Code  =  T4.CountryCode WHERE T4.IsOfficial  =  'T' AND T4.Language  =  'English')

input: question: Which unique cities are in Asian countries where Chinese is the official language ? | schema: city ( ID [PK], Name, CountryCode ) ; country ( Code [PK], Name, Continent ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language, IsOfficial ) | foreign keys: city.CountryCode -> country.Code ; countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: t1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: t2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t2 | on: t1.code = t2.countrycode || step4: SCAN | table: city | alias: t3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: t3 | on: t1.code = t3.countrycode || step6: FILTER | condition: t2.isofficial = 't' AND t2.language = 'chinese' AND t1.continent = "asia" || step7: PROJECT | columns: t3.name || step8: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: select distinct t3.name from country as t1 join countrylanguage as t2 on t1.code  =  t2.countrycode join city as t3 on t1.code  =  t3.countrycode where t2.isofficial  =  't' and t2.language  =  'chinese' and t1.continent  =  "asia"

input: question: Return the different names of cities that are in Asia and for which Chinese is the official language. | schema: city ( ID [PK], Name, CountryCode ) ; country ( Code [PK], Name, Continent ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language, IsOfficial ) | foreign keys: city.CountryCode -> country.Code ; countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step4: SCAN | table: city | alias: T3 || step5: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T3 | on: T1.Code = T3.CountryCode || step6: FILTER | condition: T2.IsOfficial = 'T' AND T2.Language = 'Chinese' AND T1.Continent = "Asia" || step7: PROJECT | columns: T3.Name || step8: DISTINCT | remove duplicates
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode JOIN city AS T3 ON T1.Code  =  T3.CountryCode WHERE T2.IsOfficial  =  'T' AND T2.Language  =  'Chinese' AND T1.Continent  =  "Asia"

input: question: Return the country name and the numbers of languages spoken for each country that speaks at least 3 languages. | schema: country ( Code [PK], Name ) ; countrylanguage ( CountryCode [PK], Language ) | foreign keys: countrylanguage.CountryCode -> country.Code
output: step1: SCAN | table: country | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: countrylanguage | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Code = T2.CountryCode || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: T1.Name | compute: COUNT(T2.Language) || step5: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 2 || step6: PROJECT | columns: COUNT(T2.Language), T1.Name
sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.Language) ,  T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(*)  >  2

sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.Language) ,  T1.Name FROM country AS T1 JOIN countrylanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code  =  T2.CountryCode GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(*)  >  2

input: question: Find the number of cities in each district whose population is greater than the average population of cities? | schema: city ( ID [PK], CountryCode, District, Population ) | foreign keys: none

sql: SELECT T1.Name ,  T2.Orchestra FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID

input: question: What are the names of conductors as well as the corresonding orchestras that they have conducted? | schema: conductor ( Conductor_ID [PK], Name ) ; orchestra ( Orchestra_ID [PK], Orchestra, Conductor_ID ) | foreign keys: orchestra.Conductor_ID -> conductor.Conductor_ID
output: step1: SCAN | table: conductor | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: orchestra | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Conductor_ID = T2.Conductor_ID || step4: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name, T2.Orchestra
sql: SELECT T1.Name ,  T2.Orchestra FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID

input: question: Show the names of conductors that have conducted more than one orchestras. | schema: conductor ( Conductor_ID [PK], Name ) ; orchestra ( Orchestra_ID [PK], Conductor_ID ) | foreign keys: orchestra.Conductor_ID -> conductor.Conductor_ID
output: step1: SCAN | table: conductor | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: orchestra | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Conductor_ID = T2.Conductor_ID || step4: AGGREGATE | group_by: T2.Conductor_ID | compute: COUNT(*) || step5: HAVING-FILTER | condition: COUNT(*) > 1 || step6: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name
sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID GROUP BY T2.Conductor_ID HAVING COUNT(*)  >  1

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID GROUP BY T2.Conductor_ID HAVING COUNT(*)  >  1

input: question: Show the name of the conductor that has conducted the most number of orchestras. | schema: conductor ( Conductor_ID [PK], Name ) ; orchestra ( Orchestra_ID [PK], Conductor_ID ) | foreign keys: orchestra.Conductor_ID -> conductor.Conductor_ID
output: step1: SCAN | table: conductor | alias: T1 || step2: SCAN | table: orchestra | alias: T2 || step3: HASH-JOIN | type: INNER | table: T2 | on: T1.Conductor_ID = T2.Conductor_ID || step4: AGGREGATE | GROUP BY [T2.Conductor_ID] || step5: PROJECT | columns: T1.Name || step6: SORT | by: COUNT(*) DESC || step7: LIMIT | value: 1
sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID GROUP BY T2.Conductor_ID ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

input: question: List the names of orchestras that have no performance. | schema: orchestra ( Orchestra_ID [PK], Orchestra, Conductor_ID ) ; performance ( Performance_ID [PK], Orchestra_ID ) | foreign keys: performance.Orchestra_ID -> orchestra.Orchestra_ID
output: step1: SCAN | table: orchestra || step2: SUBQUERY | Evaluate NOT IN subquery || step3: SCAN | table: performance || step4: PROJECT | columns: Orchestra_ID || step5: FILTER | condition: NOT Orchestra_ID IN (SELECT Orchestra_ID FROM performance) || step6: PROJECT | columns: Orchestra
sql: SELECT Orchestra FROM orchestra WHERE Orchestra_ID NOT IN (SELECT Orchestra_ID FROM performance)

input: question: Show the record companies shared by orchestras founded before 2003 and after 2003. | schema: orchestra ( Orchestra_ID [PK], Conductor_ID, Record_Company, Year_of_Founded ) | foreign keys: none
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of INTERSECT || step2: SCAN | table: orchestra || step3: FILTER | condition: Year_of_Founded < 2003 || step4: PROJECT | columns: Record_Company || step5: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of INTERSECT || step6: SCAN | table: orchestra || step7: FILTER | condition: Year_of_Founded > 2003 || step8: PROJECT | columns: Record_Company || step9: INTERSECT | Keep rows present in BOTH results (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT Record_Company FROM orchestra WHERE Year_of_Founded  <  2003 INTERSECT SELECT Record_Company FROM orchestra WHERE Year_of_Founded  >  2003

sql: SELECT Record_Company FROM orchestra WHERE Year_of_Founded  <  2003 INTERSECT SELECT Record_Company FROM orchestra WHERE Year_of_Founded  >  2003

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM Friend AS T1 JOIN Highschooler AS T2 ON T1.student_id  =  T2.id JOIN Highschooler AS T3 ON T1.friend_id  =  T3.id WHERE T2.name  =  "Kyle"

input: question: How many dogs have an age below the average? | schema: Dogs ( dog_id [PK], owner_id, breed_code, size_code, age ) | foreign keys: none

input: question: Show the property type descriptions of properties belonging to that code. | schema: Ref_Property_Types ( property_type_code [PK], property_type_description ) ; Properties ( property_id [PK], property_type_code ) | foreign keys: Properties.property_type_code -> Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code

input: question: What are the names of properties that are either houses or apartments with more than 1 room? | schema: Properties ( property_id [PK], property_type_code, property_name, room_count ) | foreign keys: none
output: step1: info: Evaluate LEFT side of UNION || step2: SCAN | table: Properties || step3: FILTER | condition: property_type_code = "House" || step4: PROJECT | columns: property_name || step5: info: Evaluate RIGHT side of UNION || step6: SCAN | table: Properties || step7: FILTER | condition: property_type_code = "Apartment" AND room_count > 1 || step8: PROJECT | columns: property_name || step9: UNION | Merge both result sets (remove duplicates)
sql: SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code  =  "House" UNION SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code  =  "Apartment" AND room_count  >  1
